page: Amazon Web Services m8gd.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by AWS Graviton processors. Built on the ARM64 architecture, the server features 48 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V2 model running at 2.8 GHz, with single-threaded execution per core. The hardware configuration includes 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with a 2850 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and it operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8gd.12xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance in memory-intensive and multi-threaded operations. Its memory subsystem achieves uncached read bandwidth of 400.62 GB/sec and write bandwidth of 208.86 GB/sec, placing it in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-core processing capabilities are highly competitive, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 23688.0 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 500.64 GB/sec. Database operations and static web serving throughput also rank in the top tier, with web serving reaching 103.55 GB/sec and Redis SET operations processing 100.66 million operations per second. Conversely, single-core performance metrics, such as the Geekbench single-core score of 1847.0, fall within the average range.

The instance offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that can leverage ARM64 architecture and local NVMe storage. The combination of dedicated physical cores, high memory bandwidth, and fast local storage makes it highly suitable for database hosting, multi-threaded software compilation, and high-throughput static web serving. However, workloads reliant on x86-specific instruction sets or those requiring high single-core performance may experience limitations on this platform.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.12xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance powered by 48 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores running at 2.8 GHz. It features 192.0 GB of RAM, a 2850 GB local NVMe SSD instance store, and a 22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers top-tier multi-core and memory performance, with uncached memory read speeds of 400.62 GB/sec and static web serving throughput of 103.55 GB/sec. While single-core benchmarks remain average, its strong multi-threaded capabilities and high-speed local storage make it highly cost-efficient for database operations, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.12xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 48 Graviton cores, 192 GB RAM, and a 2850 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.12xlarge is an ARM64 instance with 48 Graviton cores, 192 GB RAM, and 2850 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for database workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores, high-speed local NVMe storage, and exceptional multi-threaded memory bandwidth.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARM64 cores at 2.8 GHz
Includes 192.0 GB of system memory and 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers top-tier memory read bandwidth of 400.62 GB/sec
Achieves high-throughput static web serving at 103.55 GB/sec
Optimized for database hosting, multi-threaded compilation, and web serving
